sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Mr. Nelson, a 73-year-old male, presents with ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis.
He reports that his right knee has been swollen for approximately 2 months, with pain level ranging from 3 to 6 out of 10.
He has tried compression socks, which provide some relief but not complete resolution.
He experiences stiffness, particularly when getting up from a seated position, and requires a cane for support.
He has been taking Xeljanz (11 mg daily) and Celebrex (200 mg daily) for 2 months, with some improvement in symptoms before the recent increase in swelling.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis, for which he has been receiving treatment.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms related to his rheumatoid arthritis.
Current medications:
Xeljanz 11 mg daily
Celebrex 200 mg daily
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's knee is swollen, with pitting around the knee and ankle.
The patient reports pain and stiffness, particularly when straightening the knee.
Normal capillary refill in the toes.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has recurrent swelling and pain in his right knee, likely related to his rheumatoid arthritis.
Problem list: Rheumatoid arthritis; swelling of the right knee; pain of the right knee; stiffness of the right knee.
Plan:
I prescribed a steroid injection (80 mg of triamcinolone and 1 ml of lidocaine) for the patient's right knee.
I also ordered an x-ray of the knee to investigate the cause of the swelling.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to address his stiffness and mobility issues.
If the current treatment plan does not improve his symptoms, I will consider a surgery consult to discuss further management options.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again in 2-3 months to reassess his symptoms and adjust his treatment plan as needed.
I also instructed the patient to follow up with his primary care physician for routine monitoring of his CMP levels, which are currently within normal limits.
